comments.body: Implemented. Single chokepoint: added COMPLETION_REMEDIATION_GUIDANCE (generic; names the ticket-comments evidence channel; no force-close wording) and set result['remediation'] on every FAIL in reconcile_verdict (src/rebar/llm/completion.py) — so both the agentic FAIL and the deterministic child-closure FAIL (deterministic_child_failure) carry it. Surfaced it in the blocked-close error (transition_close.py) and the verify-completion text render (_llm_commands.py); documented the optional field in completion_verdict.schema.json (additionalProperties:true, non-breaking). Added 4 tests (FAIL carries guidance + names comment/evidence + no 'force' + generic; PASS has none; deterministic child FAIL carries it). Local: make lint + typecheck PASS; focused completion/close suites 60 passed; full make test running.

compiled_state.description:
# Completion verifier — coach callers to document completion evidence as ticket comments on any FAIL

## Why

When the completion verifier returns FAIL, the only remediation surfaced to the
caller is "address the criteria." There is no signposting of the legitimate,
low-friction channel for a criterion that is *already satisfied but not
discoverable from the code*: recording the supporting evidence as a comment on
the ticket. The completion verifier reads a ticket's comments (via its read-only
`show_ticket` tool) and the deterministic child-closure gate reads ticket state
directly, so documented evidence is genuinely taken into account on the next run —
but nothing tells the caller that. The result is callers reaching for the wrong
escape hatch instead of the intended evidence channel.

## What

Attach a single, generic remediation-guidance string to **every** FAIL completion
verdict, at the one chokepoint both failure paths already pass through
(`reconcile_verdict`), and surface it wherever a FAIL verdict is shown to a caller
(the close gate and the `verify-completion` text render). The guidance must:

- be generic and broadly applicable (not tied to any one incident);
- coach the caller to record evidence that meets completion requirements as
  ticket **comments** (concrete artifacts: file paths + line ranges, commands and
  their output, links, reasoning connecting the work to the criterion);
- state that the verifier reads the ticket's comments so documented evidence is
  incorporated on the next verification;
- keep the "finish genuinely incomplete work, then re-verify" route;
- NOT mention forcing a ticket closed (steer to the evidence channel only).

## Scope

- `src/rebar/llm/completion.py` — add a module-level `COMPLETION_REMEDIATION_GUIDANCE`
  constant (exported) and have `reconcile_verdict` set `result["remediation"]` on
  any FAIL verdict. Covers both the agentic FAIL and the deterministic
  child-closure FAIL (`deterministic_child_failure`), which already call
  `reconcile_verdict`.
- `src/rebar/schemas/completion_verdict.schema.json` — document the optional
  `remediation` string field (schema is `additionalProperties: true`, so this is
  contract documentation, not a breaking change).
- `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` — append the verdict's remediation
  guidance to the blocked-close error, leading with the comments-evidence channel.
- `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py` — render the remediation guidance in
  `_render_verdict_text` on a FAIL verdict.
- Tests — assert a FAIL verdict carries `remediation`, that it names the ticket
  comments channel, and that it does not mention forcing closure.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `reconcile_verdict` sets a non-empty `remediation` string on every FAIL
      verdict and leaves PASS verdicts without one (no `remediation` on PASS).
- [ ] The guidance text names the ticket **comments** channel and instructs the
      caller to document evidence (paths/commands/results/reasoning) that meets the
      completion requirements; it is generic (no incident-specific wording).
- [ ] The guidance text does not reference forcing a ticket closed.
- [ ] A blocked close (`transition <id> in_progress closed` with a FAIL verdict)
      surfaces the guidance to the caller.
- [ ] `rebar verify-completion <id>` text output shows the guidance on a FAIL
      verdict.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass locally; the change lands on
      `main` through Gerrit with `LLM-Review +1` and `Verified +1`.

file_impact.reason: add COMPLETION_REMEDIATION_GUIDANCE constant + set remediation on FAIL in reconcile_verdict
surface remediation guidance on blocked close
render remediation guidance in verify-completion text output

compiled_state.title: Completion verifier: coach callers to document completion evidence as ticket comments on any FAIL
